message:
fix(search): add ExtensionInfoLookup enum to distinguish ambiguous fuzzy matches from unique hits

find_index_entry_by_name_or_id previously returned the top-1 search hit
regardless of whether multiple entries tied on score. When two extensions
share the same fuzzy score (e.g. "foo-alpha" and "foo-beta" for query
"foo"), the old code would silently pick one, giving the user a
potentially wrong result.

The new ExtensionInfoLookup enum has three variants:
- Found(&entry): exact id/name match or unique best fuzzy hit
- Ambiguous: top-2 fuzzy hits have equal scores
- NotFound: no matches at all

handle_info_blocking now prints a distinct "is ambiguous" message with
"Try: pi search" guidance instead of showing a random match.

Includes tests:
- find_index_entry_by_name_or_id_returns_unique_fuzzy_hit
- find_index_entry_by_name_or_id_rejects_ambiguous_fuzzy_hit

Also adds test_extension_index and test_extension_entry test helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
